replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Z3 2.0/2.2i engine typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent BMW specialist) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The longitudinal six is relatively accessible in the Z3's long bonnet.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW Z3 2.0 / 2.2i — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Proactive replacement is essential on both engines
Straightforward — always worth doing; prevents catastrophic failure
Restores performance — worth it on a well-maintained Z3
Replacement often £2,500–£4,000 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW Z3 2.0 / 2.2i? Check These First
buyIf: * M54 2.2i (2000–2002) for more power and lighter aluminium block
* M52TU with documented cooling system history (water pump, thermostat, expansion tank)
* M54 with documented DISA valve and VANOS seal history
* Full service history with regular oil changes (specialist maintained)
avoidIf: * Any evidence of coolant loss or overheating — cooling system risk
* M54 with rattling from the intake — DISA valve failure risk is serious
* M54 or M52TU with loss of low-end power — VANOS seals are likely degraded
* Poor service history — both engines are unforgiving of neglect
* Rust — Z3s are valuable; body and rear subframe condition is critical

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Z3 2.0/2.2i is a charming roadster with BMW's sweet inline-six — a true driver's car. The M52TU (1999–2000) is a robust six with dual VANOS, while the M54 (2000–2002) offers more power and an aluminium block. Both share the cooling system weakness; the M54 adds the DISA valve to the list. Buy the facelift M54 for more power, maintain the cooling system proactively, and enjoy one of BMW's most characterful roadsters.

cta.label: → Compare BMW Z3 2.0 / 2.2i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW Z3 2.0 / 2.2i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle; M52TU/M54 should start smoothly
* Service History — Check the year to identify which engine — 1999–2000 = M52TU, 2000–2002 = M54
* Cooling System — Any coolant loss? Water pump, thermostat and expansion tank should be recent
* DISA Valve (M54) — Any rattling from the intake? Loss of low-end torque?
* VANOS Performance — Check low-end torque; hesitation indicates VANOS seal wear
* Oil Leaks — Any burning smell or oil spots? Oil filter housing gasket is common
* Oil Consumption — Monitor between services; both engines are generally not thirsty
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Resolve before buying
* Rust Check — Z3s are prone to rust; body, sills and rear subframe are critical

items.question: How much does a BMW Z3 2.0 / 2.2i engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned BMW Z3 2.0/2.2i engine costs £2,500–£4,000 supply only, varying by generation: M52TU £2,000–£3,500, M54 £2,500–£4,500. Fitting adds £800–£1,200. These are specialist-only replacements — the M52TU/M54 require BMW six-cylinder expertise.

items.question: Is the BMW Z3 2.0 / 2.2i reliable?
items.answer: Both the M52TU and M54 are robust, characterful sixes, but they have known weaknesses. The cooling system is the achilles heel of both engines — proactive replacement is essential. The M54 adds the DISA valve to the list of concerns. VANOS seal degradation affects both. With proper maintenance, these engines are very reliable.

items.question: What are the common problems with the BMW Z3 2.0 / 2.2i?
items.answer: Cooling system failure (water pump, thermostat, expansion tank, £400–£1,000), VANOS seal degradation (loss of low-end power, £500–£1,200), and oil leaks (oil filter housing gasket, £300–£800). M54-specific: DISA valve failure (loss of torque, rattling, £300–£600).

items.question: Is it worth replacing a BMW Z3 2.0 / 2.2i engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Z3 values range from £4,000–£15,000 depending on condition, mileage and specification. A £2,500–£4,000 reconditioned engine represents 20–80% of vehicle value — our 70% BMW scrap threshold means careful evaluation is required. For a well-maintained, low-mileage example, replacement is often justified. For a higher-mileage example with other issues (rust, bodywork), assess scrap value first. The M54 2.2i is generally more valuable and worth the investment.

items.question: How do I find my BMW Z3 2.0 / 2.2i engine code?
items.answer: The Z3 2.0/2.2i has used two engines: M52TÜB20 (1999–2000, 150bhp) and M54B22 (2000–2002, 170bhp). Check your VIN or registration year — pre-2000 is M52TU, 2000–2002 is M54. The code is stamped on the engine block.

items.question: Can I repair the cooling system issue, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — the cooling system must be proactively replaced. A water pump with metal impeller, thermostat, expansion tank and hoses costs £400–£1,000. This is the most important maintenance on the M52TU and M54. At the first sign of coolant loss, book an inspection — ignored cooling system failure can lead to catastrophic overheating and engine damage.
